channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: DOGE isn’t Silicon Valley innovation—it’s just a sloppy rebrand of free-market dogma
item.description: At a press conference in the Oval Office earlier this month, Elon Musk—a billionaire who is not, at least formally, the President of the United States—was asked how the Department of Government Efficiency manages potential conflicts of interest to ensure “accountability and transparency.
item.dc:creator: Jay Willis, Fast Company

item.title: Who at DOGE has access to U.S. intelligence secrets? Democrats are demanding answers
item.description: Democratic lawmakers demanded answers from billionaire Elon Musk’s Department of Government Efficiency on Thursday as concerns about who has access to America’s most sensitive information continue to dog Trump administration efforts to reshape the federal government.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Microsoft to Retire Skype Amid Shift to Microsoft Teams
item.description: Microsoft to retire Skype in May, shifting some services to Microsoft Teams. The tech giant prioritized Teams over Skype and aims to streamline its main communications app amidst competitors.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: NASA’s SPHEREx Space Telescope will create the world’s most complete sky survey
item.description: The sky is about to get a lot clearer. NASA’s latest infrared space telescope, SPHEREx—short for Spectro-Photometer for the History of the Universe, Epoch of Reionization, and Ices Explorer—will assemble the world’s most complete sky survey to better explain how the universe evolved.
item.dc:creator: Susan Karlin, Fast Company

item.title: Tech layoffs this year: A timeline
item.description: 2025 began in turmoil, with layoffs at some of the largest tech companies despite the support shown by the new US administration. 2024 had been a year of recovery, with the pace of layoffs slowing and IT employment the highest for years following two years of massive IT layoff in 2022 and 2023.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: R.I.P. Skype: 2003-2025
item.description: The time is up for Skype, one of the O.G.s of online communication. The once-popular 22-year-old communications software is being retired by Microsoft in May 2025, with the company’s Teams collaboration suite replacing its predecessor.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: PC sales could be set to fall this year, but Trump tariffs aren't the only reason why
item.description: Trump isn’t the only reason that PC upgrades could be getting costlier and less common, analysts say.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: How to avoid the hidden costs of onboarding
item.description: For employees, onboarding shapes their first impression of the company, helping them feel welcome and confident in their decision to join. For employers, onboarding involves numerous costs – both monetary and less quantifiable – that can make or break the bottom line.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Google’s AlloyDB is looking more and more like PostgreSQL
item.description: Google is adding new features to its fully managed database-as-a-service (DBaaS) AlloyDB in order to provide enterprises with an alternative to PostgreSQL, which has become the most popular choice when transitioning from legacy database management systems.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Apple unveils 'age assurance' technology amid child safety push
item.description: Apple will introduce "age assurance" technology to boost children's privacy and safety on the company's devices, the company announced Thursday as part of its broader push to protect kids online.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Federal agencies plan second email asking workers for weekly accomplishments
item.description: Some agencies plan to send a second Saturday email to federal workers asking them for bullet points on what they did this week, a sign at least some departments plan to continue with the controversial practice ignited by Elon Musk.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, The Hill

item.title: This tech-heavy SUV is coming to Europe –and it offers the longest all-electric range of any plug-in hybrid
item.description: An impressive new SUV from a company you probably haven't heard of is coming to Europe – meet the new Lynk & Co 08.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: How to get Novacrystal in Monster Hunter Wilds
item.description: Here's where to find Novacrystal in Monster Hunter Wilds, so that you can start crafting better armor sets and weapons.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Tech, life science companies slash hundreds more Bay Area workers
item.description: Latest layoffs affect workers in the South Bay, East Bay and SF, marking a fresh round of employment setbacks for the vital sectors.
item.dc:creator: George Avalos, The Mercury News

item.title: The Rings of Power season 3 adds Stranger Things' Jamie Campbell Bower and Heartstopper's Eddie Marsan to its cast, and I think they're perfect for two specific roles
item.description: The Rings of Power season 3's cats just got a big cast boost with the addition of two talented actors.
item.dc:creator: tom.power@futurenet.com (Tom Power), TechRadar

item.title: The iPhone 16e doesn’t have MagSafe, but apparently Apple thinks you didn’t want it anyway
item.description: The iPhone 16e is finally out, but compared to the iPhone 14 it replaces there are a few missing key features – though when it comes to MagSafe, Apple seems to think you didn’t want it in the first place.
item.dc:creator: jamie.richards@futurenet.com (Jamie Richards), TechRadar

item.title: Why fast-learning robots are wearing Meta glasses
item.description: AI and robots need data — lots of it. Companies that have millions of users have an advantage in this data collection, because they can use the data of their customers. A well-known example is Google’s reCAPTCHA v2 service, which I recently lambasted.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Prioridades de los líderes TI en materia de IA: productividad por encima de la innovación
item.description: Los líderes de TI siguen apuntando a lo más fácil cuando utilizan la inteligencia artificial (IA), centrándose en mejorar la productividad de los empleados en lugar de dar rienda suelta a usos más avanzados como permitir la innovación o aumentar los ingresos, según una nueva encuesta.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Stripe hits $91.5 billion valuation in latest tender offer
item.description: Stripe on Thursday announced a tender offer for employees and shareholders that valued the company at $91.5 billion, nearly 41% higher than its valuation a year ago, potentially delaying the fintech firm’s ambitions of going public.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: Trump promised to keep spying agencies in check. Then he fired the watchdogs he appointed
item.description: President Donald Trump vowed to fight government abuse and introduce more transparency, a stance that might align him with a little-known agency charged with watching over the U.S.’s powerful spying programs.
item.dc:creator: Alex Pasternack, Fast Company

item.title: Warren presses X on deleted Trump nominee’s posts
item.description: Sen. Elizabeth Warren (D-Mass.) is pressing the social platform X about one of President Trump’s economic nominees seemingly scraping many posts from his account. In a letter addressed to Linda Yaccarino, X's CEO, Warren asked the company to recover more than 24,000 posts from William Pulte.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: Man who said he was upset with Musk and Trump charged with threatening to burn down xAI facility
item.description: ASHLAND CITY, Tenn. (AP) — A Tennessee man has been charged with an act of terrorism after he threatened to burn down an xAI facility because he was upset with its founder, Elon Musk, and President Donald Trump, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Former Navy comptroller shares how financial management can successfully impact decision-making
item.description: Modern financial systems offer the ideal way to tie budget to strategy and improve decision-making, explains Russ Rumbaugh, who left the Navy in January 2025.
item.dc:creator: Vanessa Roberts, Federal News Network

item.title: Malware exposes 3.9 billion passwords in huge cybersecurity threat
item.description: Kurt the CyberGuy offers tips on how you can protect your devices against infostealer malware, which was used to infect 4.3 million machines last year.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Top network and data center events 2025
item.description: Ready to travel to gain hands-on experience with new networking and infrastructure tools? Tech conferences – in person and virtual – give attendees a chance to access product demos, network with peers, earn continuing education credits, and catch a celebrity keynote or live entertainment.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Autonomous robotaxi successfully completes 75 mph highway testing
item.description: Motional is developing autonomous vehicles capable of handling a wide range of real-world scenarios. Its high-speed robotaxi testing reached speeds of 75 mph.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Cot framework aims to ease Rust web development
item.description: Web developers looking for an easier time with the Rust language may want to check out Cot, an emerging web framework pitched as being for “lazy developers.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Reported cuts at NIST imperil semiconductor reshoring plans in the US
item.description: Proposed cuts at a key US government agency will likely mean the demise of CHIPS Act funding, which could effectively end the program and undermine efforts to reshore semiconductor manufacturing and research to the US.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Atlassian refocuses Trello on individual task management
item.description: Atlassian’s Trello project management tool has received a facelift with features designed to organize meetings and ideas in a cohesive manner.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: IBM acquires DataStax to boost watsonx’s generative AI capabilities
item.description: IBM has announced its intent to acquire database and related services provider DataStax for an undisclosed sum in an effort to boost watsonx’s generative AI capabilities.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Google rolls out free version of Gemini Code Assist for individuals
item.description: Google is rolling out a free version of Gemini Code Assist, its tool for code completion, code generation, and code chat, with the aim of helping individual developers improve their productivity.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: How VR technology is curing loneliness in seniors
item.description: VR transforms senior care: Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson says immersive tech helps older adults explore and connect via virtual experiences.
item.dc:creator: Fox News
